HEADLINE: EntrepreneursMeet Angelica Nwandu: Founder Of The Shade Room, Instagram's 3rd Most Engaged ProfileByTommy
SOURCE: Forbes

ARTICLE:

Small BusinessEntrepreneursMeet Angelica Nwandu: Founder Of The Shade Room, Instagram's 3rd Most Engaged ProfileByTommy Williams,Former Contributor.Forbes contributors publish independent expert analyses and insights. I write about diverse people making changeFeb 20, 2020, 04:00pm ESTMar 24, 2021, 03:57am EDTShareSaveThis article is more than 5 years old.Angelica Nwandu - Founder of The Shade Room Angelica Nwandu Traditional forms of media distribution, such as newspapers and TV, have gained much competition online, with blogs and social media platforms rising in popularity. This shift has created opportunities for entrepreneurs to tackle large, established organisations and launch media careers without possessing a traditional background in journalism. I spoke with Angelica Nwandu, 29, founder and CEO of media company and Instagram's third most actively-engaged account The Shade Room, whose business is doing just that.Pursuing Dreams And A Trip To Sundance Born in Los Angeles to Nigerian parents, Angelica faced tragedy as a child, losing her mother at the hands of her father through domestic violence at just six years old. She spent most of her childhood in an unstable foster care environment, however she joined Peace4Kids, a program for foster youth to tap into their creative potential, where she developed a love for writing. "I always knew I wanted to be a writer, even from a young age I found it extremely therapeutic and it got me through hard times" she says. Whilst she is technically African-American, her Nigerian heritage always played a role in her values and Angelica was taught by relatives and her foster parents to get a "stable career where I can look after[yourself]" she recalls. She took their advice and studied Accounting at Loyola Marymount University, looking to go into professional services. Whilst at college, a fellow student who was a writer sold their script for millions of dollars. Inspired to pursue writing again,she shared her vision with friends however was advised to focus on accounting. She continued writing whilst completing her studies and graduated with a 2.8 GPA (Grade Point Average). After graduation, Angelica joined an accounting firm to train for her CPA (Certified Public Accountant) qualification, although over time she knew that she had little passion for her vocation and still possessed dreams of becoming a writer. The Beginning Of The Shade RoomStruggling to motivate herself in her career, she decided to pursue writing again and an opportunity came up to write a script with Jordana Spiro. It was a great opportunity for someone who had not written a script before, however "the story mimicked my life so well as it was about a girl who lost her mother through domestic violence, something I truly believe was a sign from God" she recalls.To her surprise, Angelica's script was accepted into Sundance Labs, an incubator programme for film scripts that worked with mentors such as Quentin Tarintino. She took a vacation from work during tax season, a notoriously busy period for accountants. Partway through Sundance Labs, her boss called threatening to fire her if she didn't return. "I could not let this opportunity slip and decided to follow my dreams" Angelica says, although shortly after dismissing his warning, the reality of what she had committed to sank in and she was crushed. Angelica had a poetry recital later that day and broke down in tears during the performance. Ironically the audience interpreted the tears as raw emotion and she was approached by Mic who offered Angelica a $5,000 grant to write a script "which was another sign from God this was the right path".MORE FOR YOUThe Future For AngelicaOver the next few months, Angelica dedicated herself to creating her script, however her funds soon dried up and she was struggling to meet her rent obligations. During a conversation with friends, the concept of starting a celebrity blog came up. At the time, blogs were slowly gaining notoriety so Angelica settled on the idea of creating a blog where she would write articles about celebrities, news and popular culture. As she didn't have the money to launch a website of the quality she wanted, she set up the Instagram page and started blogging on the platform, and Angelica recalls that "posting stories on Instagram was not really a thing back then, as most profiles were personal". In the first post, she noticed her followers were commenting and tagging other people. Within ten days, she had 10,000 followers and knew that she was onto something. She doubled down and spent hours staying on top of stories and posting on the blog. She jokingly remembers that she would pull over on the freeway when a story broke to upload a post and ensure that she was first. After some time, celebrities started following the account and the engagement built up to a point where she had 500,000 followers. However, Angelica recalls that she "was about to get evicted so realised I needed to monetise the blog or get a job" she says and "I posted asking if any businesses would like to advertise for $75 on the platform and got multiple DMs", "ultimately we monetised out of desperation and have never actively posted since then".The Future For AngelicaToday, The Shade Room has an impressive 17.7m followers. Angelica has spent much of her time hiring the right team to support her business, which she says is particularly difficult considering most people see "The Shade Room" as a blog rather than a business. However, the team today consists of 20 people spread across writing, editorial, content and support functions, all based in Los Angeles. Angelica is proud of the team she has built, but also praises the community who have helped her achieve all of this as "most of the stories still originate from our community of roommates and we make it our duty to respond to every message".
---
